step_by_step_thinking:
During CPR, severe acidosis can be due to inadequate perfusion leading to anaerobic metabolism. Administering sodium bicarbonate can help correct metabolic acidosis by neutralizing excess hydrogen ions.

The best option for countering acidosis during CPR is D: Sodium bicarbonate intravenously. This will quickly buffer the acidic environment caused by hypoperfusion and lactic acid production.
